title: pittosporum glabrum
intro: Pittosporum glabrum, also known as the New Zealand tobacco plant, is a small shrub native to New Zealand and Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of a toxic compound called anabasine, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, and is also said to have antioxidant and antiseptic properties. Pittosporum glabrum can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, although it is essential to use caution and follow proper guidelines due to its toxicity.
